title: Anker’s last-gen sleep buds are nearly 40 percent off ahead of daylight saving time
summary: Anker's Soundcore Sleep A20 earbuds are on sale for $113.99 (40% off) with coupon code, offering an affordable sleep solution before daylight saving time disrupts rest schedules. The lightweight earbuds feature passive noise isolation, sleep tracking, and up to 14 hours of battery life to improve sleep quality.
content:
Anker’s last-gen earbuds are tiny and lightweight, making them comfy to wear while sleeping. | Image: Anker	

Bad news: most Americans are about to lose an hour of sleep next week. Good news: if you have trouble falling (or staying asleep), Newegg is currently selling Anker’s Soundcore Sleep A20 earbuds for $113.99 ($66 off) when you use coupon code MMSF88 at checkout, which drops them to just $6 shy of their lowest price to date.
Anker Soundcore Sleep A20

Where to Buy:

 $179.99 $113.99 at Newegg (with code MMSF88)

A couple of us here at The Verge are fans of Anker’s last-gen sleep buds, which do a good job of muffling disruptive noises (including snoring). They’re lightweight and comfortable enough to wear overnight, even while sleeping on your side, with multiple ear tips and wings for a personalized fit. In fact, in his review, my colleague Thomas Ricker said that they improved his average sleep time by nearly 30 minutes within a two-week period. 
What’s even more convenient is that they offer a variety of sleep-focused features to help you rest better. For example, you can use them to play a range of relaxing sounds, from meditation exercises and nature clips to white noise. You can use them as a regular pair of Bluetooth earbuds, too, just in case you prefer to listen to audiobooks or your own curated sleep playlist. They even come with adjustable EQ as well, though we wouldn’t recommend using them as your primary earbuds for music, given that they can’t match the audio quality you’d get from a pair of midrange earbuds from Apple, Sony, or Bose.
In addition, the Sleep A20 offer up to 14 hours of battery life and sleep tracking, providing insights into how long and how well you’ve slept via a companion app that also details your sleep positions and movements. The newer Soundcore Sleep A30 feature active noise cancellation, which is more effective at masking sounds than the A20’s passive isolation, but Anker’s last-gen earbuds remain a decent, budget-friendly

title: Does Ticketmaster have a stranglehold on concert ticketing — or is it just ‘bringing joy’?
summary: The Justice Department and 40 state attorneys general are suing Live Nation-Ticketmaster for creating a monopolistic 'flywheel' that forces venues and customers to stay within its ecosystem through threats and retaliation. The lawsuit alleges competitors must offer 'retaliation insurance' to venues, exposing the company's dominance over concert ticketing.
content:
In a downtown Manhattan courtroom on Monday, lawyers for the US Justice Department and 40 state and district attorneys general warned a jury that the concert industry was being squeezed by a monopolist: Live Nation-Tickemaster. After amassing dominance over ticketing and artists' use of large amphitheaters, lead DOJ counsel David Dahlquist alleged, Live Nation constructed a "flywheel" of power that forced venue customers to stay within its ecosystem. It threatened or retaliated against them when they considered leaving. To even attempt to win business, Dahlquist said, ticketing rivals needed to offer "retaliation insurance" to venues worried …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: Ticketmaster对音乐会售票是否有垄断性控制——还是只是"传播欢乐"？
zh.summary: 司法部和40名州检察长正在起诉Live Nation-Ticketmaster，指控其创建了一个垄断性的"飞轮"，通过威胁和报复迫使场地和客户留在其生态系统内。诉讼指控竞争对手必须为场地提供"报复保险"，暴露了该公司对音乐会售票的主导地位。

fr.title: Ticketmaster a-t-il une emprise sur la vente de billets de concert — ou fait-il juste "apporter la joie" ?
fr.summary: Le Département de la Justice et 40 procureurs généraux d'États poursuivent Live Nation-Ticketmaster pour avoir créé un "volant" monopoliste qui force les salles et les clients à rester dans son écosystème par des menaces et des représailles. Le procès allègue que les concurrents doivent offrir une "assurance contre les représailles" aux salles, exposant la domination de l'entreprise sur la vente de billets de concert.

de.title: Hat Ticketmaster einen Würgegriff auf den Konzertkartenverkauf — oder bringt es nur "Freude"?
de.summary: Das Justizministerium und 40 Generalstaatsanwälte der Bundesstaaten verklagen Live Nation-Ticketmaster, weil es ein monopolistisches "Rad" geschaffen hat, das Veranstaltungsorte und Kunden durch Drohungen und Vergeltungsmaßnahmen in sein Ökosystem zwingt. Die Klage behauptet, dass Konkurrenten den Veranstaltungsorten "Vergeltungsversicherung" anbieten müssen, was die Dominanz des Unternehmens beim Konzertkartenverkauf aufdeckt.

es.title: ¿Tiene Ticketmaster una estrangulación sobre la venta de entradas de conciertos — o simplemente está "trayendo alegría"?
es.summary: El Departamento de Justicia y 40 fiscales generales de los estados están demandando a Live Nation-Ticketmaster por crear una "rueda" monopolística que obliga a los lugares y clientes a permanecer dentro de su ecosistema a través de amenazas y represalias. La demanda alega que los competidores deben ofrecer un "seguro contra represalias" a los lugares, exponiendo el dominio de la empresa sobre la venta de entradas de conciertos.

title: A suite of government hacking tools targeting iPhones is now being used by cybercriminals
summary: Security researchers have discovered that exploit tools developed by governments for hacking iPhones are now being weaponized by cybercriminals, creating an emerging market for 'secondhand' exploits. This finding raises critical concerns about the proliferation of zero-day vulnerabilities and iPhone user security.
content: Security researchers say exploits used by governments to hack into iPhones have been found to be used by cybercriminals. They warned of an emerging market for "secondhand" exploits.

Cette découverte soulève des préoccupations critiques concernant la prolifération des vulnérabilités zero-day et la sécurité des utilisateurs d'iPhone.

de.summary: Sicherheitsforscher haben entdeckt, dass Exploit-Tools, die von Regierungen zum Hacken von iPhones entwickelt wurden, jetzt von Cyberkriminellen bewaffnet werden und einen entstehenden Markt für "Second-Hand"-Exploits schaffen. Diese Entdeckung wirft kritische Bedenken über die Verbreitung von Zero-Day-Schwachstellen und die Sicherheit von iPhone-Benutzern auf.

es.summary: Los investigadores de seguridad han descubierto que las herramientas de explotación desarrolladas por gobiernos para piratear iPhones ahora están siendo armadas por ciberdelincuentes, creando un mercado emergente para exploits de "segunda mano". title: No fooling: NASA targets April 1 for Artemis II launch to the Moon

title: What Trump’s war on Iran means for the US energy crunch
summary: U.S. military strikes on Iran triggered by the Trump administration's "drill, baby, drill" agenda have caused global fuel prices to surge, raising concerns about energy costs and power grid stability for Americans. While long-term impacts remain uncertain, prolonged conflict could drive higher energy prices while accelerating domestic oil and gas production.
content:
President Donald Trump arrives at the White House as joint US-Israeli military strikes on Iran continue. Washington, DC, on March 1st, 2026. | Photo: Getty Images	

Fuel prices surged after the Trump administration launched strikes against Iran on Saturday, immediately raising questions about whether the war would increase energy costs for Americans, put more pressure on power grids, and push companies to pump out more oil and gas in the US. If conflict drags on, that could potentially play into Donald Trump's plans to "drill, baby, drill" - but that doesn't necessarily protect Americans from higher energy prices. 
Keep in mind that it's still too early to tell what kind of war the US may have sparked. The spike in global oil prices could be short-lived. But prolonged conflict and disruptions to oil an …
Read the full story at The Verge.

content:
The US version of TikTok is once again experiencing issues due to an Oracle outage, just a month after coming back online from a similar outage in February. 
The service disruption, impacting Oracle's Ashburn, Virginia data center, began early Tuesday afternoon, with Downdetector reports spiking around 1PM Eastern. TikTok USDS confirmed in a post on X that US users "may temporarily experience lags in posting content while Oracle works to resolve the issue."

title: New MacBooks, the iPhone 17E, and more: Everything we know about Apple’s March 2026 announcements
summary: Apple is hosting a series of announcements throughout March 2026, including the iPhone 17E with MagSafe support, new iPad Air with M4 chip, and MacBook models with M5 chips. The company may also unveil a budget MacBook Neo powered by an iPhone chip, marking Apple's entry into the lower-cost laptop market.
content:
Apple is kicking off March with a flurry of product announcements ahead of a “special Apple experience” on March 4th in New York City, London, and Shanghai.
Apple started on March 2nd by announcing the iPhone 17E. The new $599 phone has a lot of welcome additions over the iPhone 16E, like support for MagSafe charging, doubled base storage of 256GB, and a Ceramic Shield 2 display, all for the same starting price. The same day, Apple also revealed a new iPad Air powered by an M4 chip, a bump up from the previous version’s M3 chip. March 3rd brought announcements of MacBook Pros with M5 Pro and M5 Max chips, the MacBook Air with an M5 chip, and a Studio Display refresh, including a new Mini LED-equipped XDR model.
But the company may be saving the most interesting news for last; Apple is rumored to be launching a lower-cost MacBook that’s powered by an iPhone chip instead of an M-series processor. Apple itself may have leaked the name of this new machine: It might be called the MacBook Neo.

title: Google accelerates Chrome release cycle
summary: Google is accelerating Chrome's release cycle to two weeks instead of the current four-week schedule, starting with Chrome 153 in September. The faster release cadence aims to deliver performance improvements and new capabilities more rapidly across desktop, Android, and iOS.
content:
Google is moving its Chrome browser to a two-week release cycle, instead of the current four, or the six-week cycle that existed for the decade before that. The change starts in September.
"Building on our history of adapting our release process to match the demands of a modern web, Chrome is moving to a two-week release cycle," the company said in a blog post.
The goal is to give users and developers faster access to performance improvements, fixes, and new capabilities. The smaller scope of the releases should also simplify debugging. The change applies to desktop, Android, and iOS, and begins with the stable release of Chrome 153 on Sep …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: Microsoft’s big developer conference returns to San Francisco in June
summary: Microsoft is relocating its annual Build developer conference from Seattle to San Francisco's Fort Mason venue to capture the region's AI innovation momentum. The change aims to create a more intimate event experience while positioning the conference at the center of AI industry development.
content:
Microsoft is moving its annual Build developer conference from Seattle back to San Francisco and making some changes along the way. This year, the event will be held at Fort Mason, the former US Army post located in the San Francisco Bay Area, instead of the bustling downtown of Seattle. Microsoft is moving Build to this location to capture the AI buzz of San Francisco and to make the event more intimate.
"There are great conferences that are enormous, and part of it is just the sprawl and scale of it, and there are great conferences that are tiny that are really a personalized experience," says Kyle Daigle, chief operating officer at GitHu …

title: You can soon live out your James Bond fantasies with retractable stud winter tires
summary: Finnish tire brand Nokian is launching the Hakkapeliitta 01 snow tire with studs that automatically deploy when temperatures drop, bringing James Bond-style tire technology to real cars. The innovative tires will debut in North America and Europe later this year, with a focus on Nordic markets.
content:
In 1987's The Living Daylights, James Bond presses a dashboard button to activate retractable tire studs, giving the Aston Martin V8 Vantage he's driving more traction on a frozen lake. That tire technology is no longer limited to the movies. Later this year, Finnish tire brand Nokian is  planning to launch a new snow tire with studs that automatically deploy when temperatures drop. The Hakkapeliitta 01 tire will debut in North American and Europe in a variety of sizes, with a focus on Nordic countries.
Studded tires are an effective way to improve grip and handling when driving in slippery conditions caused by ice or snow. But when roads a …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: Apple’s website leaks MacBook ‘Neo,’ which could be its new cheaper laptop
summary: A listing for Apple's rumored budget MacBook "Neo" briefly appeared on Apple's regulatory compliance page before being removed, revealing the product name and model number. The leak suggests Apple is preparing an entry-level MacBook priced under $1,000 and powered by an iPhone processor.
content:
On Tuesday, during Apple's weeklong product launch event, a listing for the "MacBook Neo (Model A3404)" appeared on a regulatory compliance page on Apple's website under its lineup of 2026 MacBooks. First spotted by MacRumors, the listing appears to be an accident and has since been removed, but may have been a leaked reference to a rumored entry-level MacBook. Unfortunately, it didn't include any additional details beyond the device's name and model number.

title: EA continues to ‘evolve’ The Sims 4 with new virtual currency and a ‘maker’ program
summary: EA is introducing a new marketplace in The Sims 4 where creators can sell items using an in-game currency called "Moola" through a new Maker Program launching March 5th. The changes mark another significant evolution for the free-to-play game, providing creators with official tools and resources.
content:
EA is adding some big changes to The Sims 4: Later this month, it's introducing an in-game marketplace where users can buy creator-made items using a new in-game currency called "Moola." The announcements mark another big shift for the game, which went free to play back in 2022.
Creators who want to sell in-game items will have to be part of the new Maker Program, which will be open to applications starting March 5th. EA will offer a "Maker Suite" that "provides official tools, resources, and clear guidelines" to ensure content made by creators works well when it's actually in the game.
On the marketplace, Makers will be able to list their …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: Big Google Home update lets Gemini describe live camera feeds
summary: Google Home's Gemini AI now features 'Live Search,' allowing users to ask real-time questions about their live camera feeds, such as 'is there a car in the driveway?' The feature is part of broader updates to the smart home platform and requires a subscription to Google Home Premium's Advanced plan ($20/month or $200/year).
content:
‘Live Search’ can describe what your cameras see, not just what they’ve seen. | Image: Google	

Google Home chief Anish Kattukaran announced several updates to the smart home platform that fix a long list of annoyances and idiosyncrasies. There's also one noteworthy new addition: the introduction of "Live Search" for your cameras.
So, instead of Gemini only knowing about things that have already happened, it now understands what it sees in your live camera feeds. That means you can ask things like, "Hey Google, is there a car in the driveway?" Live Search requires a subscription to the Advanced plan of Google Home Premium, which costs $20/month or $200/year.
Gemini for Home is now also using updated models, a change that should impro …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: Cursor据报道年化收入已超过20亿美元
zh.summary: 四岁的初创公司Cursor在过去三个月内将收入增长率翻倍,据报道已达到20亿美元的年化收入,这表明市场对AI驱动的代码编辑器需求强劲。

fr.title: Cursor aurait dépassé 2 milliards de dollars de chiffre d'affaires annualisé
fr.summary: Cursor, une startup de quatre ans, a doublé son taux de croissance des revenus au cours des trois derniers mois et aurait atteint un chiffre d'affaires annualisé de 2 milliards de dollars, signalant une forte demande du marché pour les éditeurs de code alimentés par l'IA.

de.title: Cursor hat Berichten zufolge einen jährlichen Umsatz von 2 Milliarden Dollar überschritten
de.summary: Cursor, ein vierjähriges Startup, hat seine Umsatzwachstumsrate in den letzten drei Monaten verdoppelt und soll einen jährlichen Umsatz von 2 Milliarden Dollar erreicht haben, was eine starke Marktnachfrage nach KI-gesteuerten Code-Editoren signalisiert.

es.title: Cursor ha superado los 2 mil millones de dólares en ingresos anualizados
es.summary: Cursor, una startup de cuatro años, ha duplicado su tasa de crecimiento de ingresos en los últimos tres meses y ha alcanzado 2 mil millones de dólares en ingresos anualizados, lo que señala una fuerte demanda del mercado por editores de código impulsados por IA.

title: India’s Pronto formalizes house help as its valuation jumps 8x in under a year
summary: Indian home services platform Pronto has seen its valuation jump 8x in under a year and now processes 18,000 daily bookings, leading a push to digitize India's largely offline domestic help market.
content: Pronto handles 18,000 daily bookings and plans rapid expansion as it races rivals to digitize India's largely offline home services market.

zh.title: 印度Pronto将家务帮助正式化,估值在一年内跳跃8倍
zh.summary: 印度家庭服务平台Pronto的估值在不到一年内跳跃了8倍,现在每天处理18,000个预订,领导了将印度主要离线家务劳动市场数字化的推动。

fr.title: Pronto en Inde formalise l'aide ménagère alors que sa valorisation augmente 8 fois en moins d'un an
fr.summary: La plateforme indienne de services à domicile Pronto a vu sa valorisation augmenter 8 fois en moins d'un an et traite désormais 18 000 réservations quotidiennes, menant une campagne pour numériser le marché largement hors ligne de l'aide domestique en Inde.

de.title: Indiens Pronto formalisiert Haushelfer, während seine Bewertung in weniger als einem Jahr um das 8-Fache springt
de.summary: Die indische Haushaltsdienstleistungsplattform Pronto hat ihre Bewertung in weniger als einem Jahr um das 8-Fache erhöht und verarbeitet nun 18.000 tägliche Buchungen, was einen Schub zur Digitalisierung des weitgehend offline Haushelfer-Marktes in Indien darstellt.

es.title: Pronto de India formaliza ayuda doméstica mientras su valoración salta 8 veces en menos de un año
es.summary: La plataforma india de servicios del hogar Pronto ha visto su valoración saltar 8 veces en menos de un año y ahora procesa 18,000 reservas diarias, liderando un esfuerzo para digitalizar el mercado largamente offline de ayuda doméstica de India.

title: ChatGPT uninstalls surged by 295% after DoD deal
summary: ChatGPT app uninstalls surged by 295% following news of OpenAI's Department of Defense partnership, with users expressing concerns about military AI applications. Claude saw growing downloads as users switched alternatives amid the controversy.
content: Many consumers ditched ChatGPT's app after news of its DoD deal went live, while Claude's downloads grew.

zh.title: ChatGPT卸载量在国防部交易后激增295%
zh.summary: 在OpenAI与美国国防部建立合作伙伴关系的新闻发布后，ChatGPT应用卸载量激增295%，用户对军事AI应用表示担忧。随着争议升温，Claude的下载量也在增长，用户转向寻找替代品。

fr.title: Les désinstallations de ChatGPT ont augmenté de 295% après l'accord du DoD
fr.summary: Les désinstallations de l'application ChatGPT ont augmenté de 295% suite à l'annonce du partenariat du Département de la Défense d'OpenAI, les utilisateurs exprimant des préoccupations concernant les applications militaires de l'IA. Claude a connu une croissance des téléchargements alors que les utilisateurs se tournaient vers des alternatives au milieu de la controverse.

de.title: ChatGPT-Deinstallationen stiegen nach DoD-Deal um 295%
de.summary: Nach der Ankündigung der Partnerschaft von OpenAI mit dem US-Verteidigungsministerium stiegen die Deinstallationen der ChatGPT-App um 295%, wobei Benutzer Bedenken gegenüber militärischen KI-Anwendungen äußerten. Claude verzeichnete steigende Downloads, da Benutzer angesichts der Kontroverse zu Alternativen wechselten.

es.title: Las desinstalaciones de ChatGPT aumentaron un 295% después del acuerdo del DoD
es.summary: Las desinstalaciones de la aplicación ChatGPT se dispararon un 295% tras la noticia de la asociación del Departamento de Defensa con OpenAI, con usuarios expresando preocupaciones sobre las aplicaciones militares de IA. Claude experimentó un crecimiento en descargas a medida que los usuarios cambiaban a alternativas en medio de la controversia.
